subject: Ecology
title: Life Without Sunlight
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Life Without Sunlight.

hook.text: Below 1,000 meters, the ocean is pitch black, near-freezing, and under crushing pressure.

buildup.text: No photosynthesis can happen here. Life depends on organic matter sinking from above.

discovery.text: This 'marine snow'—dead plankton and waste—feeds deep-sea communities on the seafloor.

twist.text: Some creatures wait months between meals. Metabolism slows to almost nothing.

climax.text: Deep-sea life proves that ecosystems can thrive in conditions once thought impossible.

punchline.text: No sun, no problem—if you're patient enough.

quiz.question: What is 'marine snow'?
options: Dead plankton and waste sinking from above
Actual snow that falls into the ocean
